title: 第一部分 基础训练 TEST 2

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 A
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给的 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
**Apartments for Rent!** | Apartments for Rent! | Sunshine Apartment | Alexander Apartment | | --- | --- | --- | | **Location:** | • 200 m to My Khe beach
• 300 m to An Thuong area
• 1 km to the famous supermarket—Co.opmart | • near Solbridge International School
• 3 minutes to Russian Market | | **Details:** | • Nice ocean view: Floor 12th
• 65 m² usable area: 1 bedroom, 1 kitchen and 2 bathrooms
• Modern furniture and household appliances | • 1 medium balcony
• 1 bedroom: 1 bed with soft quilts, wardrobe, and windows, TV
• 1 bathroom: water heater and washing machine
• 1 kitchen: gas stove and fridge | | **Price:** | $300/month | $270/month | | **Contact:** | Please call Stephanie Dean only on the weekend.
**Tel:** 012-440816 | Please contact the worker from the rental company at any time.
**Tel:** 012-253192 / 016-926468
**E-mail:** sopheasrun.agent@gmail.com |

questions.prompt: Renters are only able to ________ at Sunshine Apartment.
options: A. do the cooking
B. enjoy the beautiful ocean view
C. have a drink on the balcony
D. wash clothes with the washing machine
questions.translation: 租客在Sunshine Apartment只能________。
questions.explanation: 根据表中Sunshine Apartment的“Details”部分首句“Nice ocean view: Floor 12th”可知，这里可以欣赏到美丽的海景。因此答案为B。

questions.prompt: What do the two apartments have in common?
options: A. They both have two bedrooms.
B. They both rent for more than 300 dollars.
C. They are both near an international school.
D. They are both convenient for renters to go shopping.
questions.translation: 这两套公寓有什么共同之处？
questions.explanation: Sunshine Apartment附近有Co.opmart超市（1 km to the famous supermarket），而Alexander Apartment附近有Russian Market（3 minutes to Russian Market），这都方便租客购物。因此答案为D。

questions.prompt: If you want to rent Alexander Apartment, what should you do?
options: A. Call 012-440816 on a Sunday morning.
B. Contact Stephanie Dean by writing an e-mail.
C. Send an e-mail to sopheasrun.agent@gmail.com.
D. Call the worker from the rental company at 012-926468.
questions.translation: 如果你想租Alexander Apartment，你应该做什么？
questions.explanation: 根据表中Alexander Apartment的“Contact”一栏中提供了“E-mail: sopheasrun.agent@gmail.com”可知，你可以给这个邮箱发邮件。因此答案为C。

options: A. a diary
B. a report
C. an advertisement
D. a notice
questions.translation: 这篇文章最有可能是一篇________。
questions.explanation: 文章列出了两套用于出租的公寓详情、位置、价格及联系方式，显然是一篇租房广告（advertisement）。因此答案为C。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 B
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给的 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
What's Pisa? I dreamed about Pisa when I was very young. I read about the famous building called the Leaning Tower of Pisa. But when I read the word Pisa, I was thinking of pizza. I thought this tower was a place to sell pizza.

Many years later I finally saw the Leaning Tower. I knew then that it was Pisa and not pizza. But there was still something special about it for me.

The tower got its name because it really does lean to one side. Some people want to try to repair it. They're afraid it may fall over and they don't want it to lean over the city. I don't think it's a good idea to try to repair it. The tower probably won't fall down. It's more than 600 years old. Why should anything happen to it now? And if you ask me, I like what it looks like. To me it's a very humanized kind of leaning.

Nothing is perfect. And who cares? Why do people want things to be perfect? Imperfect things may be more interesting. Let's take the Leaning Tower of Pisa for example. Why is it so famous? There are many other older, more beautiful towers in Italy. But the Leaning Tower of Pisa is the most famous.

questions.prompt: The writer used to think Pisa was ________.
options: A. the same as pizza
B. not the same as pizza
C. not very famous
D. in Spain
questions.translation: 作者过去认为Pisa是________。
questions.explanation: 根据第一段“But when I read the word Pisa, I was thinking of pizza. I thought this tower was a place to sell pizza.”可知，作者以前以为Pisa就是pizza（披萨/相同）。因此答案为A。

questions.prompt: The tower got its name because ________.
options: A. it was an old building
B. it really leaned
C. it was a place to sell pizza
D. it wasn't perfect
questions.translation: 这座塔得名是因为________。
questions.explanation: 根据第三段首句“The tower got its name because it really does lean to one side.”可知，它得名是因为它确实倾斜（leaned）。因此答案为B。

questions.prompt: The Leaning Tower of Pisa is ________.
options: A. very modern
B. not popular
C. over 600 years old
D. the oldest tower
questions.explanation: 根据第三段“It's more than 600 years old.”可知，它有600多年的历史。因此答案为C。

options: A. wants to repair the tower
B. likes what the tower looks like
C. thinks it's the most beautiful tower in Italy
D. doesn't like other towers in Italy
questions.explanation: 根据第三段“And if you ask me, I like what it looks like.”可知，作者喜欢它（倾斜的）样子。因此答案为B。

questions.prompt: Why does the writer like the Leaning Tower of Pisa?
options: A. Because it's old.
B. Because it's perfect.
C. Because it sells pizza.
D. Because it's imperfect.
questions.translation: 作者为什么喜欢比萨斜塔？
questions.explanation: 根据第四段最后三句“Imperfect things may be more interesting. Let's take the Leaning Tower of Pisa for example... But the Leaning Tower of Pisa is the most famous.”可知，作者认为不完美的事物更有趣，比萨斜塔正因为它的不完美（倾斜）而最出名。因此答案为D。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 C
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给的 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
Lu Mei is an illustrator and also a cheongsam (qipao) lover. She has been drawing pictures about women in qipao for years.

Lu Mei's interest in qipao has something to do with Eileen Chang, also Zhang Ailing (1920-1995), a famous Chinese writer. In 2007, someone asked Lu if she could illustrate a book about Eileen Chang and the clothing Eileen Chang wrote about. Lu consented and started her research on the dress. Since then, she has fallen in love with drawing qipao.

Lu's book Cheongsam with beautiful illustrations shows how the dress has changed in the past 100 years. When people first wore qipao in the late 1910s, it looked like the wide robes worn by men of the time. In the 1930s, people made the dress with a close-fitting cut and higher slits.

She said, "Qipao has become a symbol of traditional Chinese culture once again, but today's dress is different from what it used to be. Because of the 2000 film In the Mood for Love, the dress now is much more close-fitting."

In Lu's everyday life, she doesn't usually wear qipao. Instead of wearing the dress, Lu hopes her readers can learn the cultural meaning behind the traditional clothing—to be independent and to keep on learning.

questions.prompt: The underlined word "consented" in Paragraph 2 probably means ________.
options: A. wondered
B. agreed
C. believed
D. remembered
questions.translation: 第二段中画线单词“consented”的意思可能为________。
questions.explanation: 根据第二段提到有人问陆梅是否能为一本书画插图，“Lu consented and started her research on the dress”以及下文“她从此爱上了画旗袍”，可推知她“同意了（agreed）”。因此答案为B。

questions.prompt:
What's the right order of Lu Mei's story with qipao?
a. Lu Mei was asked to draw pictures for a book.
b. Lu Mei wrote her own book about qipao.
c. Lu Mei fell in love with drawing pictures about qipao.
d. Lu Mei started to learn about qipao.
options: A. a-b-c-d
B. d-b-a-c
C. c-a-d-b
D. a-d-c-b
questions.translation:
陆梅与旗袍故事的正确顺序是什么？
a. 陆梅被邀请为一本书画插图。
b. 陆梅写了关于旗袍的自己的书。
c. 陆梅爱上了画关于旗袍的图画。
d. 陆梅开始了解/研究旗袍。
questions.explanation: 根据第二段：2007年陆梅被要求给书画插画（a），然后她同意并开始研究（d），随后爱上了画旗袍（c），第三段提到陆梅的书《旗袍》出版（b）。所以顺序是a-d-c-b。因此答案为D。

questions.prompt: Which of the following is TRUE about qipao in different times?
options: A. In the 1930s, qipao had lower slits.
B. People began wearing qipao in the 1920s.
C. Qipao in the 1910s looked like men's clothes.
D. Qipao now is less close-fitting after the 2000 film.
questions.translation: 关于不同时期的旗袍，以下哪项是正确的？
questions.explanation: 根据第三段“When people first wore qipao in the late 1910s, it looked like the wide robes worn by men of the time.”可知，1910年代末的旗袍看起来像当时男子穿的长袍。因此答案为C。

questions.prompt: For Lu Mei, qipao is NOT ________.
options: A. the necessary clothing in everyday life
B. a symbol of traditional Chinese culture
C. a beautiful item of clothing with about 100 years of history
D. a symbol of independence and keeping on learning
questions.translation: 对陆梅来说，旗袍不是________。
questions.explanation: 根据最后一段第一句“In Lu's everyday life, she doesn't usually wear qipao.”可知，她在日常生活中并不怎么穿旗袍，所以旗袍不是她日常生活中的必备服饰。因此答案为A。

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of the passage?
options: A. To show us the beauty of qipao.
B. To tell us how to illustrate books.
C. To encourage women to wear qipao.
D. To introduce a qipao lover and her book.
questions.translation: 这篇文章的目的是什么？
questions.explanation: 文章主要介绍了插画师、旗袍爱好者陆梅，她如何爱上画旗袍、她的插画书《旗袍》以及她对旗袍文化含义的理解。因此答案为D。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 D
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给的 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
All over the world, temperatures are going up at night because of global warming. A study found that when the temperature at night is over 30°C, the length of nightly sleep becomes shorter by over 14 minutes. This may not seem like a lot at first, but it means that each person loses 44 hours of sleep every year.

When people are short of sleep, their immune systems become weak. They get sick and forgetful, and there is even a higher risk of serious illnesses like cancer. Not getting enough sleep at night can also create an unsafe environment during the day. Those who drive to work may cause more accidents if they are not well rested.

Unluckily, women, the elderly, and the poor are most influenced by rising temperatures. Women's bodies cool quicker in the evening than men's when going to sleep, so they feel hot sooner. Older people usually sleep less because they have poor body temperature control, so sleeping in high temperatures is a real problem. In poor areas, people don't have enough money to buy fans or air conditioners. So they don't have any way to lower the temperatures in their bedrooms.

As the global warming trend is continuing, we may lose more sleep time in the future. So protecting the environment is protecting ourselves.

questions.prompt: How does the writer introduce the topic at the beginning of the text?
options: A. By listing facts.
B. By giving examples.
C. By asking questions.
D. By explaining differences.
questions.translation: 作者在文章开头是如何引入主题的？
questions.explanation: 第一段通过列举具体的研究数据和事实（温度超过30°C、每晚睡眠缩短14分钟以上、每年失去44小时睡眠）来引入全球变暖导致夜间气温上升和睡眠减少的主题。因此答案为A。

questions.prompt: What does the second paragraph mainly talk about?
options: A. The best time of going to sleep.
B. Different ways of keeping healthy.
C. Bad results of not having enough sleep.
D. The importance of staying awake during the day.
questions.translation: 第二段主要讲了什么？
questions.explanation: 第二段详细描述了睡眠不足带来的恶劣后果（免疫系统变弱、易生病且健忘、患癌症等重病风险增加、白天引发交通事故等）。因此答案为C。

questions.prompt: Why do women feel hot sooner than men when going to sleep?
options: A. Because they have more fat.
B. Because they are easier to feel nervous at night.
C. Because their bodies cool quicker in the evening.
D. Because they have poorer body temperature control.
questions.translation: 为什么女性睡觉时比男性更早感到热？
questions.explanation: 根据第三段第二句“Women's bodies cool quicker in the evening than men's when going to sleep, so they feel hot sooner.”可知，因为女性身体在晚上降温比男性快。因此答案为C。

questions.prompt: What is the structure of the text? (P = Paragraph)
questions.translation: 文章的结构是怎样的？（P = 段落）
questions.explanation: 第一段引入话题（夜间升温导致睡眠缩短），第二段说明睡眠缩短的健康危害，第三段分析哪些群体受升温和睡眠缩短的影响最大，第四段得出结论（保护环境就是保护我们自己）。四个段落是层层递进的并列/递进关系，结构为P1 -> P2 -> P3 -> P4。因此答案为A。

questions.prompt: What's the best title for the text?
options: A. Nighttime temperatures are rising
B. Global warming could mean less sleep for people
C. What can we do about global warming?
D. Who will be most influenced by global warming?
questions.translation: 本文的最佳标题是什么？
questions.explanation: 通读全文，文章主要探讨的是全球变暖导致夜间气温升高，进而使得人们的每晚睡眠缩短并带来健康和安全隐患，呼吁保护环境。因此“Global warming could mean less sleep for people”最能概括文章核心。答案为B。

sections.title: 二、任务型阅读
sections.instruction: 根据短文内容，从选项中选出能填入空白处的最佳选项。选项中有两项为多余项。
sections.passage:
Today, many kids hardly read books. [1] Developing the good habit of reading among our kids is really important to their learning success. Parents should do something to help their kids form the habit of reading. Here are some tips for kids to learn some reading skills.

**Make sure there is a reading area at home.**
Your reading space doesn't have to be big or have a lot of bookshelves. [2] A corner of the sofa is also OK. A comfortable place with enough light and room to keep a book or two can help your kid connect reading with comfort.

[3]
Reading to your kids could really help set up a good relationship. And this will become moments to be remembered when they are old and you are no longer with them.

**Bring them with you to a bookstore.**
A visit to a bookstore once a month is good reading improvement to your kids. Not only will they have more interest in books, but they will also be excited to see different kinds of books. [4]

**Read in front of your kids.**
Whether you love books, magazines, or graphic novels, let your kid see you reading. [5] If you're excited about reading, your kid is likely to catch your enthusiasm.
wordbank: Choose a quiet place to read.
Kids learn from what they see.
You should borrow some from the libraries.
It can be a chair in the room where your kid sleeps.
They are busy with their mobile phones or computers.
If there are some books they like best, buy them for your kids.

questions.answer: They are busy with their mobile phones or computers.
questions.translation: 他们正忙于玩手机或电脑。
questions.explanation: 前一句提到“Today, many kids hardly read books.（今天许多孩子几乎不读书）”，后一句提到“They are busy...（他们忙着玩手机或电脑）”，逻辑顺承说明孩子不看书的原因。对应选项F。

questions.answer: It can be a chair in the room where your kid sleeps.
questions.translation: 它可以是孩子睡觉的房间里的一把椅子。
questions.explanation: 前一句说阅读空间不需要很大（doesn't have to be big），后一句说沙发的角落也可以，此空填入“它可以是孩子卧室里的一把椅子”符合空间不需太大的逻辑。对应选项E。

questions.translation: 给他们读书。
questions.explanation: 此空为小标题。后文提到“Reading to your kids could really help...（给你的孩子读书真的可以帮助……）”，因此小标题应为“Read to them”。对应选项A。

questions.answer: If there are some books they like best, buy them for your kids.
questions.translation: 如果有他们最喜欢的书，就给孩子们买下来。
questions.explanation: 此段主题是带孩子去书店（bookstore），看到各种书很兴奋。填入“如果有他们最喜欢的书，就买给他们”切合逛书店买书的场景。对应选项G。

questions.answer: Kids learn from what they see.
questions.translation: 孩子从他们看到的东西中学习。
questions.explanation: 前一句提到“let your kid see you reading（让孩子看到你读书）”，后一句说如果你充满热忱，孩子也会受感染，说明父母的言传身教很重要，对应“Kids learn from what they see.（孩子从他们看到的学习）”。对应选项C。
